Safety Mar 29, 2026

Alaska Airlines Pilots Ask For Police “To Meet The Airplane” After Passenger Vapes Onboard

An Alaska Airlines flight from Seattle to Sacramento had to request police to meet the aircraft at the gate after a passenger allegedly vaped.

In-flight vaping incidents are escalating to law enforcement intervention, signaling airlines' reduced tolerance for vapor-related disruptions and potential cabin air quality concerns.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

I’m A Flight Attendant: This Is Why Boarding Is The Most Stressful Part Of The Job

A lot of responsibility in a short time.

Boarding's compressed timeline forces flight attendants to simultaneously manage safety compliance, passenger accommodation, and operational efficiency—creating cumulative stress that directly impacts service quality and safety outcomes.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 29, 2026

6 Military Aircraft With The Most Complex Avionics

The flying supercomputers of 2026.

Modern military aircraft avionics now integrate AI-driven sensor fusion and autonomous decision-making capabilities that far exceed traditional glass cockpit systems.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 29, 2026

Why Do Airlines Keep Older Aircraft In Service Longer Than Expected?

Older aircraft come with higher operating costs, but have unique capabilities and have already been paid off by their operator.

Airlines strategically extend aging aircraft lifecycles because fully depreciated fleets offer superior margins despite higher maintenance costs, particularly for route-specific capabilities newer aircraft cannot match.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 28, 2026

JetBlue Passenger With Highest Mosaic Status Caught Performing Inappropriate Act Inflight

The ACARS message sent during JetBlue Flight 2858 revealed a shocking act taking place in the cabin, involving a passenger in Mint.

Premium cabin disruptions now warrant real-time crew alerts via aircraft systems, escalating airline operational protocols for in-flight misconduct management.

See Simple Flying →

PAI
Safety Mar 29, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Organizations must document why residual risks remain acceptable after implementing controls, creating audit trails that demonstrate regulatory compliance under FAA safety risk management requirements.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Commercial Mar 28, 2026

After 20 Years, The Airbus A380 Still Shapes Airport Design

A closer look at some of the upgrades that airports had to make in order to accommodate the superjumbo.

A380's infrastructure legacy demonstrates how a single aircraft type can drive decades of costly airport modifications that outlast the aircraft's production era.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 28, 2026

5 Airlines With The Largest Boeing 787 Fleets In 2026

The aircraft is becoming the backbone of many airlines' fleets.

The 787's dominance in premium long-haul markets is reshaping route profitability and forcing competitors to accelerate widebody modernization efforts.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 28, 2026

Just 222 Seats: United Airlines' 4 Ultra-Premium Boeing 787 Routes In 2026

The new layout will initially be deployed domestically, with its international rollout coming next month.

Ultra-premium seating configurations signal airlines prioritizing per-seat revenue over capacity, reflecting post-pandemic demand for premium experience on transcontinental routes.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 28, 2026

5 US Airports Undergoing Major Improvement Projects

These facilities are continuing to expand.

Infrastructure investments at major US hubs signal competitive positioning as carriers prepare for post-pandemic capacity expansion and route network growth.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 29, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Organizations with genuine psychological safety in their Safety Management Systems see 3-5x higher voluntary incident reporting rates, enabling proactive hazard identification before accidents occur.

Military Mar 28, 2026

Northrop Grumman Testbed With AIM-260-Like Nose Spotted Flying Alongside Raytheon’s VOODOO 1 at Eglin AFB

The two testbeds, known by their callsigns SCAN 06 and VOODOO 1, carried out concurrent operations in Eglin’s highly instrumented airspace. For three consecutive days, on Mar. 24, 25 and 26, two testbed aircraft, the Northrop Grumman CRJ-700 carrying the U.S. registration code N806X and typically...

Concurrent testing of next-generation air-to-air missile systems by competing contractors suggests accelerated development timelines for advanced beyond-visual-range capabilities.

See The Aviationist →

Commercial Mar 28, 2026

Why Airlines Choose Certain Aircraft For Routes

An almost endless array of factors must be considered when deciding which plane should fly where...

Route selection involves balancing fuel efficiency, airport infrastructure, passenger demand, and crew scheduling—not just aircraft capacity—making optimization critical for airline profitability.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 28, 2026

Starfish Space finds a new partner for docking demonstration mission

Starfish Space has changed plans for a docking demonstration mission after its original partner backed out.

Starfish Space's ability to secure alternative partnerships quickly demonstrates growing commercial demand for on-orbit servicing capabilities despite mission delays.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 28, 2026

Germany Considering MQ-28A Ghost Bat CCA, Says Defense Minister

German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius said the MQ-28 Ghost Bat is among the uncrewed aircraft being evaluated to support the Eurofighter Typhoon fighter fleet. Germany’s defense minister Boris Pistorius said during his visit to Australia on Mar. 27, 2026, that Berlin is considering Boeing Defence...

Germany's evaluation of the MQ-28A Ghost Bat signals growing NATO adoption of autonomous combat aircraft to complement crewed fighter fleets, reshaping European air force modernization strategies.

See The Aviationist →

▶9:39
Regulatory Mar 29, 2026

FAA Part 5 SMS for Small Operators: Safety Risk Management

This video covers the safety management system (SMS) component safety risk management, which determines the need for, and adequacy of, new or revised risk controls based on the assessment of acceptable risk.

Small operators must now systematically assess and control safety risks rather than relying on reactive incident response, fundamentally shifting compliance from checking boxes to proactive hazard management.

See NBAA (YouTube) →

Military Mar 28, 2026

USMC F-35Cs and USS Tripoli ARG Arrive in the Middle East

A month after the start of the hostilities, the U.S. Marine Corps is increasing its presence in the Middle East with the arrival of a F-35C squadron and the USS Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group. Mar. 28, 2026, marks a month since the U.S. and Israel launched Operations Epic Fury and Roaring Lion,...

The accelerated deployment of carrier-based F-35Cs and amphibious assets signals a shift from reactive to sustained forward presence strategy in regional conflict zones.

See The Aviationist →

Drones Mar 28, 2026

Robinson Unmanned

Robinson Unmanned (formerly Ascent AeroSystems) is the uncrewed aircraft systems (UAS) business unit of Robinson...

Robinson's pivot to dedicated UAS operations signals how traditional rotorcraft manufacturers are structuring autonomous divisions to compete in the rapidly expanding commercial drone market.

See sUAS News →

Drones Mar 28, 2026

Quantum Systems Strengthens Ukraine’s Air Defence Capabilities with 15,000 Interceptor Contract and WIY Drones Investment

In alignment with the defence priorities set out by the Ukrainian Government, Quantum Systems has...

Quantum Systems' dual commitment of 15,000 interceptors and WIY drone investment signals commercial drone manufacturers pivoting toward defense integration as geopolitical demands reshape procurement priorities.

See sUAS News →

Regulatory Mar 28, 2026

Singapore delays SAF levy rollout amid Middle East conflict

As the war in Iran continues to roil world oil markets, Singapore has delayed the rollout of its…...

Geopolitical oil market volatility is forcing regulators to recalibrate aviation fuel tax implementation timelines, signaling that energy security concerns now compete with decarbonization priorities in policy planning.

See AeroTime Hub →

▶20:47
Regulatory Mar 29, 2026

FAA Part 5 SMS for Small Operators: Safety Policy & Safety Promotion

This video provides an overview of implementing a safety management system (SMS), specifically aligned with FAA Part 5 regulations. Join us as we explore Blue Skies Charter, a fictional company, and learn about general SMS requirements, safety policy development and safety promotion strategies. This video covers essential components like leadership commitment, gap analysis, safety objectives, emergency response planning and employee training. Whether you're a small charter operator or interested in aviation safety, this video offers valuable insights into building an effective SMS.

Small charter operators can leverage structured SMS implementation frameworks to systematically identify safety gaps and align operations with FAA Part 5 requirements without requiring extensive resources.

See NBAA (YouTube) →

Aerospace Mar 28, 2026

Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at KSC

The four Artemis 2 astronauts arrived in Florida March 27 for final preparations ahead of a launch still scheduled as soon as April 1.

The crew's arrival signals imminent execution of NASA's critical lunar return mission after years of development and delays.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 27, 2026

[Updated] Iranian Attack on Prince Sultan Air Base Reportedly Struck U.S. KC-135s, E-3 Sentry

Satellite images show signs of an attack at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia, with The Wall Street Journal now reporting that an Iranian missile damaged “several U.S. refueling aircraft.” Satellite images emerged on Mar. 27, 2026, appeared to show signs of damage on an apron used by U.S....

Damage to KC-135 tankers disrupts critical air refueling capabilities across the Middle East, forcing strategic reassessment of force projection and coalition flight operations.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

I Am Artemis: Erik Richards

Listen to this audio excerpt from Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager: For Erik Richards, supporting NASA’s first crewed Artemis mission to the Moon and back is the culmination of a career spent helping spacecraft communicate with Earth. Like many kids who grew up at the height of the...

NASA's Near Space Network infrastructure, managed by professionals like Richards, represents decades of accumulated expertise now critical to enabling lunar missions beyond Earth orbit.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon

NASA has awarded Intuitive Machines of Houston, $180.4 million to deliver NASA-funded science and technology to the lunar surface as part of the agency’s CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) initiative and Artemis program. This lunar delivery, which includes seven payloads — five of them NASA’s...

NASA's $180.4M CLPS contract to Intuitive Machines signals accelerating commercial lunar infrastructure development critical for sustained Artemis human exploration operations.

See NASA →

Industry Mar 29, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational efficiency improvements offer a near-term pathway to reduce aviation's environmental impact without waiting for new aircraft technologies to mature.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NASA’s Environment and Energy “Blue Marble” Awards Categories

Category I: NASA Environmental Quality Award Recognizes excellence in environmental management and planning, including stewardship of natural and cultural resources. This category highlights achievements in compliance, conservation, remediation, communication, and environmental information...

NASA's environmental awards program recognizes that aerospace operations must integrate ecological stewardship and resource management as core operational competencies.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

EASA warns non-airworthy stolen engine parts could re-enter the market

The European Union Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) is warning airlines, maintenance providers and parts buyers to be on…...

Stolen engine parts from scrapped aircraft pose a critical supply-chain vulnerability as criminal networks exploit maintenance loopholes to redistribute non-certified components into active fleets.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Associate Members

March 27, 2026 – Washington, D.C.—The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) is pleased to welcome Astrolab and Zeno Power, two innovative companies advancing planetary mobility and reliable energy in extreme environments. […]...

CSF's expansion to include lunar mobility and extreme-environment power suppliers signals growing industry confidence in near-term commercial space infrastructure beyond Earth orbit.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

ESA to decide by June on Europe’s Gateway contributions

MUNICH — For more than a year, questions have swirled about Artemis changes and the Gateway’s role in U.S. lunar ambitions. Following NASA’s March 24 decision to halt work on Gateway, the lunar-orbiting station that had been intended to support astronauts before and after lunar surface missions,...

Europe's delayed Gateway commitment decision signals potential fragmentation of international lunar exploration partnerships as NASA redirects Artemis priorities.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 27, 2026

Gallery: Luke Days 2026

A glimpse back at last weekend's air show at Luke Air Force Base in Arizona.

Luke Days showcases the F-35 Lightning II and modern air combat capabilities that shape pilot training and operational readiness for the future Air Force.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier

Description This image captured by U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR on Nov. 10, 2025, shows Washington’s Mount Rainier. The image is cropped from a much larger swath spanning the Pacific Northwest on a cloudy day; NISAR’s L-band SAR instrument is able to peer through the clouds at the surface...

NISAR's cloud-penetrating radar capability enables continuous Earth observation for infrastructure and hazard monitoring regardless of weather conditions, critical for aviation route planning and volcanic risk assessment.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NISAR Views Mount St. Helens

Description This image captured by U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR on Nov. 10, 2025, shows Washington’s Mount St. Helens. The image is cropped from a much larger swath spanning the Pacific Northwest on a cloudy day; NISAR’s L-band SAR instrument is able to peer through the clouds at the surface...

NISAR's L-band synthetic aperture radar enables all-weather Earth observation critical for monitoring volcanic and geological activity regardless of atmospheric conditions.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 27, 2026

NTSB Issues Preliminary Report on Hudson River Ditching

Investigators say an instructional flight ended after a loss of oil pressure and total engine failure.

Oil pressure loss leading to total engine failure highlights the critical need for enhanced engine monitoring systems and pilot training on emergency procedures in single-engine aircraft operations.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 27, 2026

NASA Tech and Science Bound for Low Earth Orbit on Commercial Launch

Technology and science demonstrations, supported by various NASA industry collaborations and agency developments, are set to launch to low Earth orbit aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket as part of the company’s Transporter-16 commercial rideshare mission. These demonstrations will test thermal...

NASA's reliance on commercial rideshare missions demonstrates the cost-effectiveness of bundling multiple payloads versus dedicated launches for technology validation.

See NASA →

Regulatory Mar 27, 2026

House Transportation Chair Sam Graves To Retire

Graves helped shape FAA reauthorization, BasicMed and several other general aviation measures.

Loss of Graves removes a key congressional advocate for general aviation regulatory flexibility at a critical juncture for industry modernization.

See AVweb →
